faqs.a: It's the giving-up response that sets in after someone faces stress they can't control over and over, until they come to believe nothing they do will make a difference. The belief can stick even after circumstances change and action would actually help.

faqs.q: Is learned helplessness the same as laziness?
faqs.a: No. It's a learned expectation built by real experiences of powerlessness, not a character flaw or a lack of willpower. Because it's learned, it can also be unlearned.

faqs.q: How is learned helplessness related to depression?
faqs.a: It has been an influential model for understanding depression, since the passivity, low motivation, and hopelessness seen in depression can look a lot like learned helplessness. It isn't itself a formal diagnosis.
